summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened by sharing encouraging stories about how God uses our pain to help others, referencing testimonies from his book. He then continued the series 'The Fire is for You,' focusing on Exodus 13:21-22, where God guided the Israelites with a pillar of cloud by day and fire by night. Pastor Travis explained that this supernatural fire symbolizes how God guides, provides, protects, and offers His light and presence to His people today. He emphasized that, just as the Israelites were led through the wilderness to the promised land, God leads us through our own dark, confusing, and uncertain seasons. The message highlighted the importance of trusting God's guidance through His Word and the Holy Spirit, listening to wise counsel to see our blind spots, and stepping out in faith even when the path ahead is unclear. Pastor Travis encouraged those feeling lost or in a 'wilderness' season to embrace it as a place where God speaks and prepares us for His promises. The sermon concluded with a call to surrender to Jesus, a prayer of salvation for those ready to take that step, and a challenge for believers to stop lying to themselves and to step out in faith where God has already made a way.
mainPoints: The pillar of fire symbolizes God's active guidance and provision for His people, leading them through darkness and uncertainty.
God's presence offers protection from seen and unseen dangers, and we must listen to the Holy Spirit and trusted community to navigate our blind spots.
The wilderness season is not abandonment but a place of divine encounter where God prepares us for His promises, calling us to step out in faith.

versesMentioned.text: By day the LORD went ahead of them in a pillar of cloud to guide them on their way and by night in a pillar of fire to give them light, so that they could travel by day or night.
"For I know the plans I have for you," declares the LORD, "plans to prosper you and not to harm you, plans to give you hope and a future."

Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or drink; or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothes? Look at the birds of the air; they do not sow or reap or store away in barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not much more valuable than they? Can any one of you by worrying add a single hour to your life?
Open my eyes that I may see wonderful things in your law.
Keep me from deceitful ways; be gracious to me and teach me your law.
When Jesus spoke again to the people, he said, "I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in darkness, but will have the light of life."
Keep your lives free from the love of money and be content with what you have, because God has said, "Never will I leave you; never will I forsake you."

Monday.scripture: Exodus 13:21-22 - "By day the LORD went ahead of them in a pillar of cloud... by night in a pillar of fire."
Monday.action: Identify one decision you are facing. Ask God for clear guidance and commit to looking for His direction throughout the day.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for going ahead of me. Please guide my steps today and make your path clear. Help me to trust and follow you. Amen.

Tuesday.scripture: Psalm 119:18 - "Open my eyes that I may see wonderful things in your law."
Tuesday.action: Spend 10 minutes reading a Psalm. Ask God to open your eyes to one specific truth you need to see today.
Tuesday.prayer: God, open the eyes of my heart. Help me to see your truth in your Word and apply it to my life. Keep me from deception. Amen.

Wednesday.scripture: Psalm 119:29 - "Keep me from deceitful ways; be gracious to me and teach me your law."
Wednesday.action: Ask God to reveal one area where you have been lying to yourself. Write it down and confess it to Him.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, keep me from lying to myself. Show me the truth about my heart and my situation. Grant me your grace and teach me your ways. Amen.

Thursday.scripture: Hebrews 13:5 - "Never will I leave you; never will I forsake you."
Thursday.action: In a moment of quiet, meditate on God's promise that He will never leave you. Recall a past time when you felt His presence.
Thursday.prayer: Thank you, Jesus, for your promise to never leave me. Help me to feel your presence today, especially if I feel alone or in the wilderness. I trust in you. Amen.

Friday.scripture: John 8:12 - "I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in darkness."
Friday.action: Turn off all distractions for 5 minutes. Ask Jesus, the Light of the World, to illuminate one area of confusion or darkness in your life.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, you are my light. I choose to follow you today. Shine your light on my path and dispel any darkness or fear in my heart. Amen.

Saturday.scripture: Matthew 6:25-27 - "Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life..."
Saturday.action: Identify one worry you are carrying. Actively choose to release it to God and take one practical step of faith instead of worry.
Saturday.prayer: Heavenly Father, I confess my worry about [mention it]. I choose to trust you as my provider. Help me to step out in faith, not fear, today. Amen.
